titel: A law regarding public service broadcasting and guidelines for its operations for the period 2026–2033.
html:
The government proposes consolidating regulations regarding public service broadcasting into a new law – the "Law on Public Service." This law will include provisions regarding the public service mandate, the financing of this mandate, the review of programs, and certain rules for appointing board members and the CEO of public service companies.

The government also proposes amendments to the Radio and Television Act (2010:696). These amendments stipulate that a broadcasting license for a public service company should no longer be subject to conditions other than those specifically related to broadcasts over the terrestrial network. The government also proposes repealing the law (2018:1893) concerning the financing of radio and television in the public interest.

Furthermore, the government proposes expanding the obligation to retransmit content to include related services that provide access to subtitles, interpretation, text-to-speech, or similar technologies.

In addition, the government proposes legal amendments that are a consequence of other proposed legislation.

The new law and the amendments to the Radio and Television Act are proposed to come into effect on December 2, 2025. However, these laws are proposed to be applied for the first time to the public service mandate, broadcasting licenses, and the public service fee pertaining to periods after December 31, 2025, and to programs broadcast or provided after the same date. Other legal amendments are proposed to come into effect on January 1, 2026.

The government is also submitting proposals regarding the content of the public service mandate for the period 2026–2033, as well as the allocation of funds for the activities included in this mandate.

Proposed Resolutions for the Parliament

Prop. 2024/25:166

Government's Proposals:

  1. The Parliament shall approve the government's proposed law on public service broadcasting.
  2. The Parliament shall approve the government's proposed amendment to the law (1991:1559) containing provisions related to the Freedom of the Press Act and the Freedom of Expression Act.
  3. The Parliament shall approve the government's proposed amendment to the law (2002:562) on electronic commerce and other information society services.
  4. The Parliament shall approve the government's proposed amendment to the Radio and Television Act (2010:696).
  5. The Parliament shall approve the government's proposed amendment to the law (2011:1244) on tax procedures.
  6. The Parliament shall approve the government's proposed amendment to the law (2018:1937) on accessibility to digital public services.
  7. The Parliament shall approve the government's proposed amendment to the law (2022:818) on the provision of data by the public sector.
  8. The Parliament shall approve the government's proposed amendment to the law (2023:407) on important public announcements.
  9. The Parliament shall approve the government's proposed amendment to the law (2023:664) on media support.
  10. The Parliament shall approve what the government proposes regarding the content of the public service broadcasting mandate for the upcoming period (section 6).
  11. The Parliament shall approve what the government proposes regarding the framework for the operations (section 7).
  12. The Parliament shall approve what the government proposes regarding how the content will be broadcast and provided (section 8).
  13. The Parliament shall approve what the government proposes regarding how the content mandate will be designed (section 9).
  14. The Parliament shall approve what the government proposes regarding accessibility for people with disabilities (section 10).
  15. The Parliament shall approve what the government proposes regarding the follow-up of the public service broadcasting mandate (section 11).
  16. The Parliament shall approve what the government proposes regarding management and organization (section 12).
  17. The Parliament shall approve what the government proposes regarding preparedness and security (section 13).
  18. The Parliament shall approve what the government proposes regarding the financial conditions (section 14).
tldr: The government aims to create a new law that consolidates all regulations regarding public service media into a single document. The proposal also addresses how programs will be funded and how they will be made accessible to everyone until the year 2033.
lattlast:
The government has proposed a new law called the "Public Service Act." The aim is to consolidate all regulations regarding the public service mandate, how funding should be allocated, and how programs should be reviewed into a single law. This is intended to provide greater clarity for organizations that provide public service, such as SVT and SR.

The government also wants to amend other laws to simplify the regulations. A key aspect is that those producing programs must ensure they are accessible to everyone. This means that technologies such as subtitles, interpretation, and text-to-speech should be included in the regulations governing the distribution of programs. This will make it easier for people with disabilities to access the information.

The proposal also includes regulations on how the work should be managed and how security should be handled. This includes, for example, how to handle important public service announcements during crises. The government also wants to determine the amount of funding that will be allocated to the operations during the period from 2026 to 2033.

If the parliament approves the proposal, the new regulations will come into effect in December 2025. However, the new rules regarding the content and funding of programs will not be implemented until after December 31, 2025. The remaining amended laws are proposed to come into effect on January 1, 2026.
